extraction.one_line_summary: LEKNESSUND Andreas attacked from breakaway with 18km to go, gained 10 seconds on peloton over final climb to win by 38 seconds and move to 7 seconds on GC
tactical_patterns: Ineos/Bora/Quick-Step chase cut 6-minute break to 90 seconds but abandoned chase with 18km remaining, conceding stage.
GC teams miscalculated effort needed when Leknessund gained time uphill rather than losing it in final 18km.
Break survivor attacked with 18km out—not summit—exploiting peloton's hesitation between stage and GC control.

rider_signals.LEKNESSUND Andreas: positive: TT specialist gained time climbing in final 18km, suggesting evolved climbing ability beyond palmares
rider_signals.BETTIOL Alberto: positive: won peloton sprint easily despite mistaking it for stage win, exceptional form pre-Tour
rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: negative: dropped before final classified climb on hilly but non-mountain stage
rider_signals.KRISTOFF Alexander: negative: eliminated on same terrain as Sagan, concerning weakness for versatile sprinter
rider_signals.WILLIAMS Stephen: neutral: defended yellow without incident but Ineos prioritized controlling margin over stage
